back_translation: The leaders of the people settled in Jerusalem, and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one out of ten to dwell in Jerusalem, the holy city, while nine-tenths remained in their own cities.
The people blessed all the men who volunteered to live in Jerusalem.

These are the heads of the province who lived in Jerusalem. (In the towns of Judah, the Israelites, priests, Levites, temple servants, and descendants of Solomon's servants each lived on his own property in their cities —

while in Jerusalem some of the descendants of Judah and Benjamin lived.) From the descendants of Judah: Athaiah son of Uzziah, son of Zechariah, son of Amariah, son of Shephatiah, son of Mahalalel, a descendant of Perez;
and Maaseiah son of Baruch, son of Col-hozeh, son of Hazaiah, son of Adaiah, son of Joiarib, son of Zechariah, a descendant of Shelah.
All the descendants of Perez who lived in Jerusalem totaled 468 men of valor.
These are the descendants of Benjamin: Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Joed, son of Pedaiah, son of Kolaiah, son of Maaseiah, son of Ithiel, son of Jeshaiah;
followed by Gabbai and Sallai — 928 in total.
Joel son of Zichri was the officer over them, and Judah son of Hassenuah was over the second district of the city.
From the priests: Jedaiah son of Joiarib; Jachin;
Seraiah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub, the chief of the house of God.
and their associates who did the work of the temple — 822 men; Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pelaliah, son of Amzi, son of Zechariah, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah;
and his associates, heads of families — 242 men; Amashai son of Azarel, son of Ahzai, son of Meshillemoth, son of Immer;
and their associates — 128 men of valor. Zabdiel son of Haggedolim was their officer.
From the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, son of Bunni;
Shabbethai and Jozabad, leaders of the Levites, who oversaw the outside work of the house of God;
Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zabdi, son of Asaph, the leader in thanksgiving and prayer; Bakbukiah, second among his associates; and Abda son of Shammua, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun.
All the Levites in the holy city totaled 284.
And the gatekeepers: Akkub, Talmon, and their brothers who kept watch at the gates — 172 men.
The rest of Israel, with the priests and Levites, were in all the cities of Judah, each on his own inheritance.
The temple servants lived on the hill of Ophel, with Ziha and Gishpa over the temple servants.
The overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi son of Bani, son of Hashabiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Mica, of the descendants of Asaph, the singers, who oversaw the service of the house of God.
For there was a royal decree concerning them, an ordinance for the singers daily.
Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, a descendant of Zerah son of Judah, was the king's representative in every matter concerning the people.
As for the villages with their fields, some of the descendants of Judah lived in Kiriath-arba, Dibon, Jekabzeel, and their villages;
in Jeshua, Moladah, and Beth-pelet;
in Hazar-shual, in Beersheba and its villages;
in Ziklag, in Meconah and its villages;
in En-rimmon, in Zorah, in Jarmuth;
in Zanoah, Adullam, and their villages; in Lachish and its fields; and in Azekah and its villages. They settled from Beersheba all the way to the Valley of Hinnom.
The descendants of Benjamin from Geba lived in Michmash, Aija, and Bethel with its villages;
in Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah;
Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim;
Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat;
Lod, Ono, and the Valley of the Craftsmen.
And some divisions of the Levites who were in Judah also settled in Benjamin.
